Who We Are

A Nigeria and Africa where every older adult receives quality healthcare

MAHI Africa — the Mapa Healthy Ageing Initiative — was founded in 2025 at Federal Teaching Hospital Katsina. We address the catastrophic gap in healthcare access for elderly Africans in conflict-affected, rural, and underserved communities where ageing populations are growing fastest yet served least.



9.4M
Nigerians aged 60 and above today
28M
Projected by 2050 — the system is not ready
"Africa is ageing. The system is not ready. MAHI Africa is starting the work."
88+
Elderly persons screened in inaugural community outreach
5
Active research studies on geriatric health in Northwest Nigeria
4
Pillars of integrated community health intervention
2025
Founded at Federal Teaching Hospital Katsina
From the Field

Our Work in Action

Inaugural MAHI Africa Community Outreach — Batagarawa, Katsina State, 2025

Our Four Pillars

How We Work

I
🏘️

Community Outreach and Screening

Direct engagement with elderly populations in rural and conflict-affected communities. Blood pressure screening, glucose testing, and health education — reaching those who have never been screened before.

II
🎓

Health Worker Training and Capacity Building

Training community health workers in geriatric-focused clinical skills, elderly communication, and NCD identification — adapted for low-resource, high-insecurity settings.

III
🩺

Donation of Medical Supplies and Equipment

Targeted donation of essential diagnostic and therapeutic supplies to facilities serving elderly populations, paired with tracking systems to ensure equipment reaches patients.

IV
🌍

Rural-Proofing and Community Adaptation

Every protocol is designed for the realities of conflict-affected Northern Nigeria: unreliable electricity, insecurity, limited literacy, and cultural norms around ageing.

5
Active geriatric research studies as Principal Investigator
Meet the Founder

Dr. Philemon Kojo Woode

Dr. Kojo Woode is a medical doctor completing housemanship at Federal Teaching Hospital Katsina, where witnessing preventable deaths from uncontrolled NCDs in elderly patients drove him to found MAHI Africa in 2025.

As FAMSA President (2021–2022), he represented African medical students at the WHO African Region Regional Committee — where WHO Regional Director Dr. Moeti's call to address Africa's geriatric crisis crystallised the mission that became MAHI Africa.

Ready to Support Elderly Health Equity in Africa?

Whether you are a donor, funder, health professional, or community advocate — there is a place for you in MAHI Africa's work.

About MAHI Africa

Who We Are

A first-mover organisation dedicated to elderly health equity in conflict-affected Northern Nigeria, building the evidence base and community systems that did not exist before.

Our Story

Born at the Bedside

MAHI Africa was founded in 2025 by Dr. Philemon Kojo Woode during his housemanship at Federal Teaching Hospital Katsina. Month after month, more than 80% of deaths in the Medicine Department were attributable to preventable complications of undetected and uncontrolled NCDs in elderly patients.

These were not deaths caused by incurable disease. They were deaths caused by a system not designed for its ageing population — where elderly persons were told to go home and die rather than pursue treatment. Where there were no geriatricians, no screening programmes, no community health workers trained in elderly care.

MAHI Africa was created to change that — starting with Katsina, scaling to Nigeria, reaching the continent.

2025
Year Founded
FTHK
Federal Teaching Hospital Katsina
Katsina → Nigeria → Africa
Our scale ambition: from community to continental
🎯
Vision

Every Older Adult, Everywhere

A Nigeria and an Africa where every older adult has access to quality healthcare, regardless of geography, conflict status, financial capacity, or the assumptions others make about the value of their lives.

⚡
Mission

Community-Driven, Evidence-Based

MAHI Africa improves healthcare access, disease prevention, and health equity for elderly populations in Northern Nigeria through community outreach, health worker capacity building, medical supply delivery, and evidence-based advocacy.

🔄
Theory of Change

From Undetected to Controlled

Trained CHWs reach elderly persons, screen them using donated equipment, connect them to medication supply, and generate data that drives policy — reducing undetected NCD and emergency presentations to tertiary facilities.

The Founder

Dr. Philemon Kojo Woode

Dr. Philemon Oritsetsolayemi Kojo Woode holds an MBBS from the University of Abuja (2024) and a BSc Biochemistry from Delta State University (2011). He is currently completing housemanship at Federal Teaching Hospital Katsina, where MAHI Africa was born.

As FAMSA President (2021–2022), he represented African medical students at the 71st and 72nd WHO African Region Regional Committee sessions. It was at the 71st session, where WHO Regional Director Dr. Moeti spoke on Africa's critical shortage of geriatricians, that his commitment to elderly health equity was crystallised.

He was selected from over 31,000 applicants across 41 African countries for the Tech4Africans AI and Machine Learning Scholar Cohort 6, with a 95% fee waiver. He holds five AI certifications through Microsoft Elevate, PoliSync, and TechCrush.

He is Principal Investigator on five active research studies on geriatric health, and has been recognised as Best House Officer in the Department of Surgery and Outstanding House Officer by the Association of Resident Doctors at FTHK.

University of Abuja

MBBS, 2024

Delta State University

Our Programs

Where We Work

Four interlocking pillars of intervention, grounded in community-level delivery and designed for the realities of conflict-affected Northwest Nigeria.

I
🏘️

Community Outreach and Screening

Direct engagement with elderly populations in rural and conflict-affected communities. MAHI Africa teams deploy to community health centres to conduct blood pressure screening, blood glucose testing, and structured health education.

Our inaugural outreach in Batagarawa screened 88 elderly persons for hypertension and diabetes — the majority for the first time in their lives.

Blood Pressure Screening Glucose Testing Health Education
II
🎓

Health Worker Training and Capacity Building

Training of community health workers and primary healthcare staff in geriatric-focused clinical skills, elderly communication, and NCD identification. Training is adapted for low-resource, high-insecurity settings.

CHWs become the sustainable long-term link between elderly communities and the health system, conducting regular home visits and monitoring patients who cannot travel.

CHW Training Geriatric Skills Home Visits
III
🩺

Donation of Medical Supplies and Equipment

Targeted donation of essential diagnostic and therapeutic supplies to facilities serving elderly populations. The inaugural donation to Comprehensive Health Centre Batagarawa included five sphygmomanometers, two AccuCheck glucometers, drugs, and consumables.

All donations are paired with a tracking system to ensure equipment reaches elderly patients rather than remaining unused.

Sphygmomanometers Glucometers Drug Supply
IV
🌍

Rural-Proofing and Community Adaptation

Every MAHI Africa protocol, tool, training manual, and digital system is designed for the realities of conflict-affected Northern Nigeria: unreliable electricity, insecurity, limited literacy, and cultural norms around ageing.

No intervention is considered viable unless it can function in a community where a CHW visit may be an elderly person's only healthcare contact in a given month.

Low-Resource Design Conflict Settings Cultural Adaptation
Flagship Project

Digital Community Hypertension Management

The HTN Treatment in Nigeria programme demonstrated treatment rates above 90% and BP control above 50% among 21,000 patients. MAHI Africa extends this proven model specifically to elderly populations in conflict-affected communities where no equivalent programme currently exists.

Months 1–3

Establish and Baseline

App development, CHW training, baseline data collection in Batagarawa. Stakeholder engagement with Katsina State PHC Authority. Target: 100 elderly persons enrolled, CHWs certified, app operational.

Months 4–6

Expand and Deepen

Expand to 2 additional communities. Pharmaceutical partnerships for drug supply. Second CHW training cohort. Target: 300 elderly persons enrolled across 3 communities.

Months 7–12

Measure and Advocate

Monitor BP control rates and referral completion. Analyse data. Produce policy brief. Present to Katsina State Ministry of Health. Target: 40% improvement in BP control rates.

Get Involved

Be Part of the Solution

MAHI Africa is at a critical early stage. Your partnership, funding, or collaboration directly determines how many elderly Africans receive care they would otherwise never access.

💰

Fund Our Work

MAHI Africa has demonstrated proof of concept and alignment with the world's most significant frameworks on healthy ageing. Your funding directly enables community screening, CHW training, and medical supply donations.

  • Seed and programme grants
  • Equipment and supply donations
  • Research funding partnerships
  • Drug drive contributions
🤝

Become a Partner

We actively seek partnerships with academic institutions, international NGOs, government bodies, pharmaceutical partners, and health technology organisations that share our commitment to elderly health equity in Africa.

  • Academic and research collaborations
  • Implementing partner agreements
  • HelpAge International network
  • Pharmaceutical supply chain partners
📣

Advocate and Amplify

Help us build visibility for elderly health equity in Africa. Share our work, connect us with funders, invite us to conferences, and help ensure that the fastest-growing demographic on the continent is no longer invisible.

  • Conference speaking invitations
  • Media and press coverage
  • Funder introductions
  • Social media amplification
